author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Added 34 focused unit tests on `resolve_divergence`'s
per-field reducer, covering every branch named in the
ticket's matrix.

The path required a small enabling refactor: the pure reducer
was buried inside `resolve_one`, which loads each head's
`(entries, record, comments)` via `JjRepo`. Extracted the
pure portion as `pub(crate) fn reduce_to_merged(id, snapshots)`,
plus the `HeadSnapshot` struct that was inline. Behavior is
byte-identical; `resolve_one` is now a thin adapter that
fetches snapshots from jj and calls the helper. Tests
construct `HeadSnapshot`s directly with hand-built
`HistoryEntry`s and `Op` variants — no jj shell-out, no
test-repo scaffolding, each test < 60 lines.

Coverage now in `merge_ops.rs`'s `#[cfg(test)]`:
- LWW + 3 tie-breakers (`Jjf-At`, commit hash, `trailer_index`)
  for `status`, `title`, `assignee`, `body`, and (bonus) `type`
  and `slug`. The unstamped-loses-to-stamped invariant exercised
  via the resolver, not just the OpKey direct-comparison.
- `SetAssignee { assignee: None }` LWW path (the unassign
  operation, separate from "no op present").
- `body`: hash → bytes resolution from head records; the
  "winning hash missing on any head" error path; the no-SetBody
  fallback that pulls from rendered records.
- Labels and deps: add/no-op, add-then-remove (LWW),
  idempotent same-label both heads, and the cross-side
  add/remove/add scenarios.
- Comments: union by id with `created_at` ordering, plus the
  defensive same-id-on-both-heads dedup.
- Cross-version: both heads using unstamped (v1-trailer)
  Create + ops mixed with stamped (v2-trailer) ops.
- Structural: shared-pre-fork dedup by `(commit, trailer_index)`,
  and the chain-without-create error path.

# Recommendation

Land it. The `sync-reenable-merge-cross-check` ticket
(`0896e4f`) now has a unit-test surface for pinning any
divergence the re-enabled cross-check surfaces; new policy
regressions break CI in milliseconds (the new tests run in
~0.3s combined) instead of through the slow integration
loop.

One **surprising finding** worth flagging to the spec: the
inline comment on `PrimaryKey` claims "pre-bump stanzas sort
before stamped ops at the same second." The implementation is
**wider** than that — derived `Ord` on the variant-order
declaration `Unstamped(_) < Stamped(_)` makes every unstamped
op sort before every stamped op, regardless of clock time. Pinned
by `cross_version_any_stamped_beats_any_unstamped_regardless_of_clock`.
This is the right real-world semantics (pre-bump ops predate
post-bump ops by construction in real data), but the
spec-comment is narrower than the code. Two reasonable next
moves: tighten the comment to match the code, or change the
key to compare on a unified projected timestamp. Filed as a
follow-up below; not blocking.

# Confidence

High that every branch named in the ticket's matrix is
covered. Medium that the test suite catches future regressions
in pathological cases I didn't think to test (e.g. ties on the
`trailer_index` across heads — impossible in practice since
each head's commits are distinct). The reducer is small and
the tests are direct; a coverage tool would corroborate but
isn't necessary.

# Open follow-ups

- Spec/comment alignment ticket: pin whether `Unstamped <
  Stamped` is the intended semantics (and tighten the inline
  comment), or whether the key should compare on a unified
  projected timestamp (currently the implementation favors v2
  even when v2 is clock-earlier than v1).
- Unblocks `sync-reenable-merge-cross-check` (`0896e4f`) per the
  ticket's "BLOCKS" relationship.
